name: Prof. Dr. Frank Glorius
description: Group leader driving research at the intersection of organic chemistry, data science, and machine learning for chemical discovery.

name: Dr. Ajnabiul Hoque
description: Deep learning and reinforcement learning for enantioselective C–H activation reactions, generative AI for molecular design, and ML-guided reaction optimization with experimental validation.

name: Dr. Felix Katzenburg
description: Developer of pyGecko for calibration-free GC-MS/FID quantification and automated high-throughput reaction screening. Co-developed evolutionary algorithms for interpretable molecular representations.

name: Florian Boser
description: Laboratory automation and data-centric approaches to autonomous chemical discovery. Contributed to the Schlenkputer platform for autonomous air-sensitive reactions, and works on strain-release photocatalysis and high-throughput screening.

name: Niklas Dobberstein
description: Generative transformer models for de novo molecular design (LLamol) and autoregressive crystal structure generation (Materium). Focuses on multi-conditional molecular generation and interpretable molecular representations.

name: Niklas Hölter
description: Photocatalysis and synthetic methodology, including phenothiazine sulfoxide photocatalysts and photocatalytic iminothiolation. Previously co-authored work on standardized substrate selection strategies for unbiased evaluation of reaction generality.

name: Maik Niedziella
description: Radical philicity prediction using machine learning, high-throughput automated GC-MS methods, and phosphine-catalyzed annulation reactions.

name: Debanjan Rana
description: Energy transfer catalysis and data-driven screening strategies for dearomative cycloadditions. Co-developed the EnTdecker platform and co-authored work on standardized substrate selection for unbiased reaction evaluation.

name: Leon Schlosser
description: Lead developer of EnTdecker, an ML-based platform for substrate discovery in energy transfer catalysis. Works on inverse photocatalyst design, machine learning for enantioselective organocatalysis, and dearomative cycloaddition discovery.

name: Jan Spies
description: Energy transfer photochemistry, including Brook rearrangement-enabled synthesis of gamma-amino alcohols. Background in NHC chemistry for microelectronic applications and molecular prosthetics for urea cycle disorders.

name: Manh Hung Tran
description: Robustness screening, automated substrate screening, and chemical space analysis to systematically evaluate reaction performance, accelerate discovery, and guide data-driven optimization across diverse molecular libraries.

name: Nick Wierich
description: Photoredox catalysis and radical chemistry, including alkyl radical generation from boronic esters via aminyl radical substitution. Background in strained molecule chemistry and bicyclo[1.1.0]butane reactivity.
